sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: The DHS strongly pushed back on the report, calling it an "absolute ludicrous lie and unequivocally false."
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: The article structurally foregrounds DHS and Coast Guard denials while only briefly summarizing the Washington Post's documentary evidence. The phrase 'strongly pushed back' adds editorial emphasis to the denial side. Sonnet missed this structural bias.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: Later on Thursday, the Coast Guard told The Post that it would look into the new policy changes. "We will be reviewing the language," Coast Guard spokesperson Jennifer Plozai told the paper.
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: This crucial detail — that the Coast Guard acknowledged the need to review policy language, which actually supports the Post's reporting — is buried near the end of the article. The structural placement de-emphasizes information that undermines the categorical denials that dominate the piece. Sonnet missed this.

gpt_false_negatives.biasedText: The DHS strongly pushed back on the report, calling it an "absolute ludicrous lie and unequivocally false."

gpt_false_negatives.biasedText: Later on Thursday, the Coast Guard told The Post that it would look into the new policy changes. "We will be reviewing the language," Coast Guard spokesperson Jennifer Plozai told the paper.
gpt_false_negatives.reason: This significant detail — that the Coast Guard itself acknowledged the need to review its policy language — undercuts the categorical denials dominating the article, yet it is buried near the end with minimal emphasis. GPT missed this structural bias.
